name: Push/Pull/Legs (PPL)
description: High frequency split training each muscle group twice weekly. Push days train chest, shoulders, and triceps. Pull days train back and biceps. Leg days train quads, hamstrings, and glutes. Based on Schoenfeld 2016 meta-analysis showing 2x/week frequency is superior for hypertrophy.

name: Upper/Lower Split
description: Balanced 4-day program hitting upper body and lower body twice per week. Combines power work (3-5 reps) and hypertrophy work (8-12 reps) across sessions. Great for intermediate lifters with 4 days available.

name: Full Body 3x/Week
description: Efficient program hitting all muscle groups each session with compound movements. Perfect for beginners building foundational strength. Based on ACSM recommendations for novice trainees.

name: Bro Split
description: Classic bodybuilding split with one muscle group per day and high per-session volume. Each muscle gets 20-25 sets of focused work. Research shows equal hypertrophy to higher frequency when total weekly volume is matched.

name: PHUL
description: Power Hypertrophy Upper Lower. Combines strength (3-5 reps) and hypertrophy (8-12 reps) across 4 days. Power days build neural strength, hypertrophy days maximize muscle growth. Best of both worlds.

name: PHAT (Layne Norton)
description: Power Hypertrophy Adaptive Training by Dr. Layne Norton. Advanced 5-day program with speed sets on hypertrophy days. Demands solid foundation in compound lifts. Includes deload every 3rd week.

name: 5/3/1 (Wendler)
description: Periodized strength program with 4-week mesocycles. Uses Training Max (85-90% of 1RM) for sustainable progression. Week 1: 5s, Week 2: 3s, Week 3: 5/3/1, Week 4: Deload. Add 5lb upper/10lb lower per cycle.

name: Starting Strength / StrongLifts 5x5
description: Linear progression for beginners using 5 compound lifts. Add 5lb every successful session. Works for 3-6 months before plateaus require intermediate programming. Builds rapid neural adaptations.

name: German Volume Training
description: 10 sets of 10 reps at 60% 1RM with 60-90s rest. Extreme volume hypertrophy protocol. Run for 4-6 weeks maximum during caloric surplus. Recovery demands are very high.
